description: Issuer Q&A response on the Birchal communication facility that gives a substantive, balanced, factual answer to a real investor question. Should pass ADV-017.
text:
Investor question (posted by user @rachel_n on the Birchal Q&A facility):

> What's your customer-acquisition cost on the Brookvale hub today, and how does it compare to the major chains?

Response from Sarah Wilkinson, FarmThru co-founder & director:

Good question, Rachel.

In Q1 2026 our blended customer-acquisition cost was $42 per acquired household, measured across paid social, local print and word-of-mouth referral. The figure is calculated as total marketing spend in the quarter divided by net new paying households on a 30-day-active basis.

We don't have direct visibility into the major chains' CAC, but published industry estimates put it in the low single digits per shopper, mostly because they advertise from a position of pre-existing brand presence. Our figure is materially higher and we expect it to remain so until our brand recognition catches up. The full CAC trend by quarter and the marketing-spend assumptions behind our forecasts are in the financial section of the offer document.

Happy to dig into any specific channel.

Sarah
